channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Trump Administration Finalizes Narrower Anti-Discrimination Rules for U.S. Lenders
item.description: The Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB) in the United States is preparing to finalize a set of regulations that would reduce the scope of long-standing anti-discrimination obligations for lend...

item.title: Government Employees to Work Half-Day from Home on Holy Wednesday
item.description: The Palace has announced that all government employees will be on a half-day work-from-home (WFH) arrangement this Holy Wednesday, March 31. This directive aims to provide flexibility for employees du...

item.title: Parliament Supports Fort Hare Council in Suspending Vice-Chancellor Amid Governance Concerns
item.description: Parliament’s portfolio committee on higher education and training has officially endorsed the University of Fort Hare council’s decision to place vice-chancellor Prof Sakhela Buhlungu on precautionary...

item.title: U.S. Supreme Court Reviews Case on Citizenship for Children Born in the Country
item.description: The U.S. Supreme Court is currently examining a significant case regarding birthright citizenship, which guarantees citizenship to most children born on U.S. soil. This case revolves around an executi...

item.title: DOTr to Implement Free and Discounted Rides for Commuters This Holy Week
item.description: Commuters in the Philippines may get some relief this Holy Week as the Department of Transportation (DOTr) plans to offer subsidized or free rides. Transportation Secretary Giovanni Lopez explained th...

item.title: Iyabo Ojo steps back from mediating Funke Akindele and Toyin Abraham feud
item.description: Actress and filmmaker Iyabo Ojo don yan about the wahala between her colleagues, Funke Akindele and Toyin Abraham, wey happen for the premiere of her film 'The Return of Arinzo.' Video wey show Akinde...

item.title: Man convicted after XL bully attack leads to pensioner's death
item.description: A 31-year-old man, Sean Garner, has been convicted for owning an XL bully dog that fatally attacked 84-year-old John McColl in Warrington, Cheshire. The attack occurred on 24 February last year, after...

item.title: Israeli Ambassador Declines Apology to Family of Australian Aid Worker Killed in Gaza
item.description: On the eve of the second anniversary of Lalzawmi 'Zomi' Frankcom's death, Israel's ambassador to Australia, Hillel Newman, declined to apologise for the Australian aid worker's killing, citing 'uninte...

item.title: NRI TDP Members Across the Gulf Celebrate Telugu Desam Party’s 44th Foundation Day
item.description: The overseas members of the Telugu Desam Party (TDP) enthusiastically celebrated the party’s 44th Formation Day, showcasing solidarity among Non-Resident Indians (NRIs) in the Gulf region. The NRI TDP...

item.title: Almost Half of South Africa’s Wastewater Systems Classified as Critical
item.description: South Africa’s municipal water and sanitation systems are facing serious challenges, as revealed in the 2025 Green Drop report and related assessments. Nearly half of the country’s wastewater systems ...

item.title: Kwara South Residents Criticise Governor AbdulRazaq for Absence Amid Rising Banditry
item.description: Residents for different communities inside Kwara South don dey para well-well as dem accuse Governor AbdulRahman AbdulRazaq say im abandon dem during serious bandit wahala. Reports talk say di governo...

item.title: Increasing Number of Canadians Delay CPP Pension to Age 70
item.description: Historically, few Canadians postponed the start of their Canada Pension Plan (CPP) benefits beyond age 65. For years, the majority opted for early access, driven by the 'bird in the hand' mindset, whi...

item.title: Understanding the Roots and Persistence of Antisemitism
item.description: In this opinion piece, Sheryl Saperia reflects on the enduring presence of antisemitism throughout history and its resurgence in contemporary society. She draws parallels between the historical suffer...

item.title: Government introduces temporary fuel levy cuts to ease April price increases
item.description: South Africans will face steep increases in fuel prices from Wednesday, 1 April 2026, as global tensions and a weaker rand drive costs higher. Mineral and Petroleum Resources Minister Gwede Mantashe a...

item.title: El-Rufai Returned to ICPC Custody After Bail Hearing
item.description: Malam Nasir El-Rufai, former governor of Kaduna State, don return to the custody of the Independent Corrupt Practices and other related Commission (ICPC). Dem release am earlier on March 27, 2026, aft...

item.title: PM Shehbaz reviews fuel supply chain, orders measures against smuggling and hoarding
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif on Tuesday directed tough measures to curb the smuggling and illegal hoarding of petroleum products amid ongoing global fuel supply disruptions caused by the Middle East ...

item.title: Balancing KiwiSaver contributions with household affordability
item.description: KiwiSaver, New Zealand’s retirement savings scheme, is set to increase default contributions from 3% to 3.5% for both employees and employers starting April 1, with a further rise to 4% planned for 20...

item.title: Rajnath Singh criticises Congress for neglect of Assam, urges voters to support BJP
item.description: Union Defence Minister Rajnath Singh on Tuesday strongly criticised the Congress party for what he described as decades of neglect towards Assam, urging voters not to forgive the party in the upcoming...

item.title: DCI Investigation Finds Negligence, Not Murder, in Kericho Mass Grave
item.description: The Directorate of Criminal Investigations (DCI) has clarified that the discovery of human remains in a mass grave in Kericho is not linked to murder. According to DCI Director Mohamed Amin, the inves...

item.title: Key Concerns of Coimbatore South Voters Ahead of Assembly Elections
item.description: Coimbatore South Assembly constituency, which will witness a four-cornered contest on April 23, includes major commercial hubs and prime residential areas of Coimbatore city, spread across ten Corpora...
